text: This video describes the technique of radical apical lymph nodes dissection guided by carbon nanoparticles in laparoscopic rectal cancer surgery. This is a sixty-five-year-old man presented with rectal bleeding. The lean is ten centimeters from the anal verge. Preoperative staging shows a three-three rectal cancer. One day before surgery, the patient received a submucosal injection of one milliliter atavit carbon nanoparticle suspension beside the tumor under colonoscopy via a XXIII-G needle catheter.

Under laparoscopic view, five trocars are used for access. The tumor and mesentery lymph nodes are stained black, aiding in identification. The surgeon performs radical apical lymph nodes dissection using a median to lateral approach, with clear visualization and protection of the superior hypogastric nerve. The colon mesentery is dissected from median to lateral, and the stained apical lymph nodes are carefully dissected while ensuring nerve protection.
The surgeon continues the dissection of the apical lymph nodes towards the cranial side, maintaining a clear view of the stained nodes and surrounding structures.

The surgeon isolates and estranges the inferior mesenteric artery, performing high ligation and cutting it. The dissection continues laterally, exposing, ligating, and cutting the inferior mesenteric vein.
Six apical lymph nodes are harvested during the laparoscopic procedure, with no visible lymph node metastases. The final pathology indicates stage IIa rectal cancer.
In concluding, carbon nanoparticles is effective as a tracer for enhanced intraoperative identification and dissection of apical lymph nodes in laparoscopic resection for rectal cancer.
